text.title: ops-contractor-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ops-contractor-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Giving feedback at monthly cadence instead of same-week.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Behavioral connection weakens beyond one week. Contractors cannot recall the specific micro-decision that produced the outcome; feedback becomes abstract criticism.

failure-mode.description: LLM-generated feedback drifts toward mealy-mouthed praise without specific behavior + measurable impact.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents default to positive framing. Force SBI structure and require at least one concrete behavior + one measurable outcome in every feedback block.

failure-mode.description: Agent over-trusts time-tracker self-reports without cross-checking against PR/commit/asset volume.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Time tracking is vulnerable to gaming. Before passing hours to evaluation, cross-check reported time against verifiable output volume.

failure-mode.description: Agent digests Slack history and flattens sarcasm and friction signals.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any thread with more than 5 back-and-forth messages indicates friction that requires human reading, not summarization.

failure-mode.description: Agent auto-paying invoices or auto-approving payment without human review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-border payments on disputed work are hard to claw back. Each invoice requires explicit human approval against the SOW.

failure-mode.description: Agent pulling Slack or Notion into LLM context without redaction of customer data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Status threads frequently reference customer names, deal sizes, or support issues. Use a redaction step before any external-API LLM call.

failure-mode.description: Auto-generated SOW addenda re-introducing default IP/confidentiality clauses that conflict with the master agreement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always diff any agent-generated addendum against the signed master agreement before sending.
